title: Peronocactus minimus
intro: Peronocactus minimus, also known as the dwarf Peronocactus, is a small, cacti species native to Mexico. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and inflammation, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which provide potential health benefits such as improved gut health and reduced oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Peronocactus minimus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Peronocactus minimus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders.
taxonomy: Peronocactus minimus is classified as a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is a small, globular cactus with white, woolly spines and small, star-shaped flowers. Peronocactus minimus is native to Mexico and is often found growing in rocky outcrops and dry scrublands. The species is also known as Mammillaria minimus, and its classification can vary depending on the source. Peronocactus minimus is a member of the Peronocactus genus, which is a group of cacti that are closely related to the genus Mammillaria.
